question: Do the egg yolks exhibit a semi-fluid, jammy consistency with a gooey, flowing center?
Do the yolks appear freshly sliced with realistic translucence, rather than looking solid or hard-boiled?
Do the nori sheets display a crispier appearance with pronounced surface texture and irregularities?
Are visible micro-pores or fine porous details present on the surface of the nori sheets?
Is the color of the nori sheets maintained as a natural dark tone, avoiding unnatural vivid green hues?
Are the position and orientation of the egg halves identical to the original image?
Is the outer shape of the egg whites preserved, maintaining the original halved oval silhouette without distortion?
Is the steam rising from the bowl completely preserved in shape, density, and visibility?
Are the oil highlights and specular reflections on the broth surface preserved without smearing or removal?
Are the noodles identical in arrangement, color, and thickness to the original image?
Is the dark broth color and consistency maintained without lightening or thickening?
Are the chopsticks, spoon, and ceramic bowl preserved with no changes to their appearance or position?
Is the image free from unnatural artifacts, halos, or blurring around the modified egg yolks?
Is the image free from noise, pixelation, or pasted-on texture artifacts on the nori sheets?
Does the lighting on the modified elements (yolks, nori) match the lighting consistency of the rest of the scene?
